key_facts: Creech beat to death a fellow inmate, David Dale Jensen, in the maximum security tier of the Idaho State Correctional Institution.
Creech had four prior murder convictions when he killed Jensen.
Creech claimed responsibility for killing twenty-six people, and the bodies of eleven of his victims were later recovered in seven states.
Creech was charged with first-degree murder for killing Jensen.
Creech pleaded guilty to the charge.
Dr. Floyd LaMarr Heyrend, a psychiatrist, testified that Creech had antisocial personality disorder and acted impulsively without adequate forethought and sufficient consideration for others.
legal_issues: Ineffective assistance of counsel at resentencing
Procedurally defaulted claims under Martinez v. Ryan
Duration of confinement and cruel and unusual punishment under the Eighth Amendment
decision: The district court denied the habeas corpus petition and the appeals court affirmed the decision.
reasoning_summary: The appeals court agreed with the district court that the Idaho Supreme Court reasonably found a lack of prejudice under the second prong of Strickland. The court also held that the sub-claims at issue were not procedurally defaulted new claims and that Martinez did not apply. The court denied COAs as to the first, second, and fourth uncertified issues.
